summary: The Mk7 Golf R shares the GTI's excellent EA888 Gen 3 engine with a larger IS38 turbo and Haldex AWD. It's similarly reliable but adds the complexity of the AWD system and higher stress from more power.

issues.name: Haldex AWD Pump and Filter
issues.description: The Haldex coupling requires fluid and filter changes every 20,000 miles. Neglected maintenance leads to pump failure and loss of AWD functionality.

issues.name: Carbon Buildup on Intake Valves
issues.description: Direct injection carbon buildup is common across all EA888 engines. The Golf R's higher boost levels may accelerate the process slightly.

issues.name: DSG Clutch Pack Wear
issues.description: The DSG clutch packs can wear under high-torque situations, especially on tuned cars. Shuddering during low-speed maneuvers is the first sign.

issues.name: Water Pump Failure
issues.description: Same water pump issue as the GTI. The electric water pump can fail and cause coolant leaks. VW extended warranty coverage on affected models.

issues.name: Intercooler Cracking (Plastic End Tanks)
issues.description: The factory intercooler has plastic end tanks that can crack under boost pressure, especially on tuned cars. Causes boost loss and coolant ingestion.

buyingTips: Haldex fluid and filter changes every 20,000 miles are critical — verify service records include this
The plastic intercooler end tanks are the Golf R's biggest weakness — upgraded aluminum intercoolers solve this permanently
DSG fluid changes every 40,000 miles prevent expensive mechatronic and clutch pack issues
Carbon cleaning at 50,000 miles is recommended — plan for it as part of ownership
Many Golf Rs have been tuned — ask directly about modification history and check for aftermarket parts
